Unit 6-7: Adjectives & Questions

This exercise is divided into four fluency-building activities to move you from theory to automaticity.

1. The Constrained Picture Exercise

(Two people looking at colorful fresh fruit at an outdoor Greek market under the sunlight)

Power Lexis:

Greek Pronunciation English
Φρέσκος, -η, -ο (frés-kos) Fresh
Κόκκινος, -η, -ο (kó-ki-nos) Red
Πόσο (pó-so) How much
Κάνει (ká-ni) It costs (literally "it makes")

Grammar Tags (Goals): 1. Ask a question using the essential phrase Πόσο κάνει; 2. Use an adjective agreeing in gender with its noun.

Pro Tip: In Greek, adjectives must always match the gender (masculine, feminine, or neuter), number, and case of the noun they describe.

2. Audio-Visual Shadowing

Listen to the native speaker describing the scene. Your goal is to speak at the exact same time, mimicking the rhythm and speed.

Click to reveal Transcript «Αυτό το φρούτο είναι κόκκινο και φρέσκο. Πόσο κάνει;» (This fruit is red and fresh. How much does it cost?)

Why it works:

  • κόκκινο και φρέσκο: The noun "το φρούτο" is neuter. Therefore, the adjectives "κόκκινος" and "φρέσκος" must drop their masculine "-ος" ending and take the neuter "-ο" ending to become "κόκκινο" and "φρέσκο".
  • Πόσο κάνει;: This is the standard, everyday way to ask for the price of a singular item in Greece.

3. The Mutation Drill (Speed-Shift)

Read the "Base" sentence aloud. Then, look at the "Trigger" and instantly change the sentence mentally before checking the Target. This builds agility without overthinking.

Base Sentence Trigger Target Sentence
Το κόκκινο μήλο κάνει δύο ευρώ.
(The red apple costs two euros.)		 Change subject to Ο καφές (Masculine) Ο κόκκινος καφές κάνει δύο ευρώ.
Πόσο κάνει η μικρή τσάντα;
(How much does the small bag cost?)		 Change subject to Το βιβλίο (Neuter) Πόσο κάνει το μικρό βιβλίο;
Το φρέσκο νερό είναι κρύο.
(The fresh water is cold.)		 Change subject to Η σαλάτα (Feminine) Η φρέσκια σαλάτα είναι κρύα.
Έχω ένα μεγάλο πρόβλημα.
(I have a big problem - Neuter)		 Change object to Η ιδέα (Feminine) Έχω μία μεγάλη ιδέα.

4. The Grammar-Saturated Story

Read this short story three times: first for accuracy, second for comprehension, and third as fast as possible. This forces your brain to "soak" in the patterns of adjectives and basic questions.

«Το μεγάλο σπίτι έχει μία κόκκινη πόρτα. Η μικρή πόρτα είναι ανοιχτή. Ποιος είναι εκεί; Ένας καλός φίλος είναι εκεί. Τι θέλει; Θέλει λίγο κρύο νερό. Πόσο κάνει το κρύο νερό; Είναι δωρεάν!»
